Question: How does Paul describe the people he is writing to in this letter?
Response: Paul describes the people he is writing to as saints, and as faithfully trusting in Christ Jesus.

Question: With what has God the Father blessed believers?
Response: God the Father has blessed believers with every spiritual blessing in the heavenly places in Christ.

Question: When did God the Father choose those who believe in Christ?
Response: God the Father chose those who believe in Christ before the foundation of the world.

Question: For what purpose did God the Father choose the believers?
Response: God the Father chose the believers so they could be holy and blameless before him.

Question: Why did God destine the believers in advance for adoption?
Response: God destined the believers in advance because he was pleased to do it.

Question: Why did God destine the believers in advance for adoption?
Response: God destined the believers in advance so that he would be praised for his glorious grace.

Question: What do believers receive through the blood of Christ, God’s beloved one?
Response: Believers receive redemption, the forgiveness of their trespasses through the blood Christ.

Question: What will God do when the times for the completion of his plan come?
Response: God will bring together everything in heaven and on the earth under Christ.

Question: What seal did the believers receive when they heard the word of truth?
Response: The believers were sealed with the promised Holy Spirit.

Question: Of what is the Spirit a down payment?
Response: The Spirit is a down payment of the inheritance of believers.

Question: What does Paul pray the Ephesians will be enlightened to understand?
Response: Paul prays that the Ephesians will be enlightened to understand the hope of their calling and the glorious riches of Christ’s inheritance in the saints.

Question: What did the same power that now works in the believers do in Christ?
Response: The same power raised Christ from the dead and seated him at God’s right hand in the heavenly places.

Question: What has God put under Christ’s feet?
Response: God has put all things under Christ’s feet.

Question: What is Christ’s position of authority in the Church?
Response: Christ is the head over all things in the Church.

Question: What is the Church?
Response: The Church is Christ’s body.

Question: What is the spiritual condition of all unbelievers?
Response: All unbelievers are dead in their trespasses and sins.

Question: Who is working in the sons of disobedience?
Response: The ruler of the authorities of the air is working in the sons of disobedience.

Question: By nature, what are all unbelievers?
Response: All unbelievers are by nature children of wrath.

Question: Why is God rich in mercy towards believers?
Response: God is rich mercy because of his great love.

Question: By what have believers been saved?
Response: Believers have been saved by God’s grace.

Question: Where are believers seated?
Response: Believers are seated in the heavenly places with Christ Jesus.

Question: For what purpose has God saved and raised up believers?
Response: God has saved and raised up believers so that in the ages that are coming he might show them the great riches of his grace.

Question: How have we been saved?
Response: We have been saved by grace through faith as God’s gift.

Question: Why should believer not boast?
Response: No believer should boast because he is not saved by his own works.

Question: For what purpose has God created believers in Christ Jesus?
Response: God’s purpose for believers in Christ Jesus is that they should walk in good works.

Question: What is the spiritual condition of unbelieving Gentiles?
Response: Unbelieving Gentiles are separated from Christ, alienated from Israel, strangers to the covenants, without hope and without God.

Question: What has brought some Gentile unbelievers near to God?
Response: Some Gentile unbelievers have been brought near to God by the blood of Christ.

Question: How did Christ change the relationship between Gentiles and Jews?
Response: Christ made believing Gentiles and Jews into one group, destroying the hostility that divided them.

Question: What did Christ abolish in order to make peace between Jews and Gentiles?
Response: Christ abolished the law of commandments and regulations in order to make peace between Jews and Gentiles.

Question: By what means do all believers have access to the Father?
Response: All believers have access to the Father by means of the Holy Spirit.

Question: On what foundation is God’s family built?
Response: God’s family is built on the foundation of the apostles and prophets, Christ Jesus being the cornerstone.

Question: What kind of building are believers becoming?
Response: They are becoming a holy temple for the Lord.

Question: Where does God dwell in the Spirit?
Response: God dwells in the Spirit within believers.

Question: For whose benefit did God give Paul his stewardship?
Response: God gave Paul his stewardship for the benefit of the Gentiles.

Question: What was made known to Paul?
Response: The revelation about the mystery was made known to Paul.

Question: To whom has God revealed what was not made known to the human race in other generations?
Response: God revealed the hidden truth about Christ to his apostles and prophets.

Question: What hidden truth has been revealed?
Response: The hidden truth revealed is that the Gentiles are fellow heirs and fellow members of the body, and fellow sharers in the promise in Christ Jesus.

Question: What gift was given to Paul?
Response: The gift of God’s grace was given to Paul.

Question: About what was Paul sent to help the Gentiles understand?
Response: Paul was sent to help the Gentiles understand the administration of the mystery that was hidden from the ages in God.

Question: Through what will the complex wisdom of God be made known?
Response: Through the church the complex wisdom of God will be made known.

Question: What does Paul say believers have because of faith in Christ?
Response: Paul says believers have boldness and access with confidence because of faith in Christ.

Question: What is named and created after the Father?
Response: Every family in heaven and on earth is named and created after the Father.

Question: How does Paul pray for the believers to be strengthened?
Response: Paul prays for the believers to be strengthened with power through God’s Spirit.

Question: What does Paul pray that the believers will be able to understand?
Response: Paul prays that the believers will be able to understand how wide and long and high and deep is the love of Christ.

Question: What does Paul pray will be given to the Father to all generations?
Response: Paul prays that the glory in the Church and in Christ Jesus will be given to the Father to all generations.

Question: How does Paul urge believers to live?
Response: Paul urges believers to walk in a way that is worthy of their calling.

Question: What did Christ give to each believer after his ascension?
Response: Christ gave each believer grace according to the measure of the gift of Christ.

Question: What five types of people does Paul say Christ gave?
Response: Christ gave apostles, prophets, evangelists, pastors, and teachers.

Question: What are these five types of people supposed to do for the church?
Response: These five types of people are supposed to equip the believers for the work of service, for the building up of the body.

Question: How does Paul say believers can be like children?
Response: Believers can be like children by being tossed around and carried away by people’s trickery and erring deceit.

Question: How does Paul say the body of believers is constructed?
Response: The body of believers fits together, held together by each joint, each part working in the body’s growth, for the building up of each one in love.

Question: How does Paul say the Gentiles walk?
Response: The Gentiles walk in the futility of their minds.

Question: What does Paul say has happened to the understanding of the Gentiles?
Response: The understanding of the Gentiles has been darkened.

Question: To what have the Gentiles handed themselves over?
Response: The Gentiles have handed themselves over to sensuality to do every kind of impurity.

Question: What does Paul say believers must put off?
Response: Believers must put off what belongs to the old man.

Question: What does Paul say believers must put on?
Response: Believers must put on the new man.

Question: To whom must a believer never give opportunity?
Response: A believer must never give opportunity to the devil.

Question: What must believers do instead of steal?
Response: Believers must labor so that they may be able to share with the person who has need.

Question: What kind of talk does Paul say must come out of the believer’s mouth?
Response: No corrupt talk must come out of the believer’s mouth, but, instead, words that build up others.

Question: Who must a believer not grieve?
Response: A believer must not grieve the Holy Spirit.

Question: What is a believer to do because God in Christ forgave him?
Response: A believer must forgive others because God in Christ forgave him.

Question: Who should believers imitate?
Response: Believers should imitate God the Father as his children.

Question: What did Christ do which was a pleasing aroma to God?
Response: Christ gave himself up for the believers as an offering and sacrifice to God.

Question: What must not be suggested among believers?
Response: Sexual immorality, impurity, and greed must not be suggested among believers.

Question: What attitude should instead be seen among believers?
Response: Believers should instead have an attitude of thankfulness.

Question: Who has no inheritance in the kingdom of Christ and God?
Response: The sexually immoral, impure, and greedy have no inheritance in the kingdom of Christ and God.

Question: What is coming upon the children of disobedience?
Response: The wrath of God is coming upon the children of disobedience.

Question: What fruit of the light is pleasing to the Lord?
Response: The fruit of goodness, righteousness, and truth is pleasing to the Lord.

Question: What are believers to do with the works of the darkness?
Response: Believers are not to participate with, but instead expose, the works of darkness.

Question: What is revealed by the light?
Response: Everything is revealed by the light.

Question: What should believers do since the days are evil?
Response: Believers should redeem the time since the days are evil.

Question: What leads to recklessness?
Response: Getting drunk with wine leads to recklessness.

Question: With what should believers speak to each other?
Response: Believers should speak to each other with psalms, hymns, and spiritual songs.

Question: In what way should wives be subject to their husbands?
Response: Wives should be subject to their husbands as to the Lord.

Question: Of what is the husband the head, and of what is Christ the head?
Response: The husband is head of the wife, and Christ is head of the Church.

Question: How does Christ sanctify the Church?
Response: Christ sanctifies the Church holy by the washing her of water with the word.

Question: How should husbands love their wives?
Response: Husbands should love their wives as their own bodies.

Question: How does a person treat his own body?
Response: A person nourishes and loves his own body.

Question: What happens when a man is joined to his wife?
Response: When a man is joined to his wife, they become one flesh.

Question: What hidden truth is demonstrated by the joining of a man and his wife?
Response: The hidden truth about Christ and his Church is demonstrated by the joining of a man and his wife.

Question: How should Christian children treat their parents?
Response: Christian children should obey their parents.

Question: What are Christian fathers to do for their children?
Response: Christian fathers are to raise their children in the discipline and instruction of the Lord.

Question: With what attitude should Christian slaves obey their masters?
Response: Christian slaves should obey their masters in the honesty of their heart as for the Lord.

Question: What should a believer remember about whatever good deed he does?
Response: A believer should remember that for whatever good deed he does, he will receive a reward from the Lord.

Question: What should a Christian master remember about his Master?
Response: A Christian master should remember that his and his servant’s Master is in heaven, and that there is no favoritism with him.

Question: Why must a believer put on the whole armor of God?
Response: A believer must put on the whole armor of God to stand against the wicked plans of the devil.

Question: Against whom does a believer battle?
Response: A believer battles against governments and rulers of the realm of darkness and against spiritual forces of evil.

Question: Why must a believer put on the whole armor of God?
Response: A believer must put on the whole armor of God to stand against the wicked plans of the devil.

Question: Which piece of the armor of God puts out the flaming arrows of the evil one?
Response: The shield of faith puts out the flaming arrows of the evil one.

Question: What is the sword of the Spirit?
Response: The sword of the Spirit is the word of God.

Question: How should believers conduct themselves in prayer?
Response: Believers should pray at all times, persevering and watching for God’s answer.

Question: What does Paul wish to have through the prayers of the Ephesians?
Response: Paul wishes to have the word given to him with boldness when he speaks the gospel.

Question: Where is Paul as he writes this letter?
Response: Paul is in chains in prison as he writes this letter.

Question: What does Paul ask that God the Father and the Lord Jesus Christ give the believers?
Response: Paul asks that God will give them peace and love with faith.
